rulings.Case Title: MARTIN ENG VS. MIKE O'REILLY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 28, 2013, Line 1, DEFENDANT DAN NOLTING's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained, with 20 days leave to amend. As to the first cause of action, Mr. Nolting's communications with law enforcement are barred by the litigation privilege (Hagberg v. California Federal Bank FSB (2004) 32 Cal.4th 39, 364), and Mr. Eng is granted leave to amend to re-allege, if he can do so in good faith, the other defamatory statements made by Mr. Nolting that were included in his original Complaint. As to the second cause of action, Mr. Eng is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege an economic relationship with a third party. As to the third cause of action, Mr. Eng is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege a cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress. As to the fourth cause of action, Mr. Eng is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege actual damages from the trespass. As to the fifth cause of action, Mr. Eng is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege a cognizable cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: COALITION FOR ADEQUATE REVIEW VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Otion Fo Rreasonalbe Attorneys Fees On Petitioners Motion To Strike
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 28, 2013, Line 2, PETITIONERs COALITION FOR ADEQUATE REVIEW, NINTY-NINE PERCENT, ROB ANDERSON's Motion For Reasonable Attorneys Fees On Petitioners Motion To Strike. Granted in part. The hearing will be at 9 a.m., not 9:30 a.m. Petitioners are awarded $16,000 as reasonable fees for their counsel's work in successfully moving to strike the City's costs memorandum and in bringing this fees motion. Petitioners are entitled to fees per CCP 1021.5 because they had been successful in their initial writ petition which met the statutory criteria and the City's costs memorandum was related to that petition. There is no reason to depart from the $200 hourly rate for Ms. Miles that was determined on the petitioners' previous motion for fees heard and decided last year. The hours spent by Ms. Miles to defeat a $52,000 costs memorandum were disproportionate to the amount at stake. Moreover, those hours were excessive given that the motion to strike essentially raised two issues - whether the City was the prevailing party such that it was entitled to a costs award and, if so, whether the costs it claimed were reasonably incurred - were fairly simple and did not require extensive legal research or analysis. 60 hours of time spent by a $200 per hour attorney is the most amount of time that is reasonable to have spent in preparing the moving and reply papers (no hearing was held on the motion) and thus $12,000 is awarded for that work. The hours spent by Ms. Miles to seek fees for her work on the motion to strike are also disproportionate and excessive. The issues on this motion are routine and not difficult. 20 hours of time spent by a $200 per hour attorneys is the most amount of time that is reasonable to have spent in preparing the moving and reply and thus $4000 is awarded for that work. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The City is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of its counsel in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. The hearing will be at 9am, not 9:30am. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THE NATIONAL COLLECTION AGENCY, INC., A VS. MICHAEL BINNINGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion And Motin To Quash Ntc Of Levy And Or Recover
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 28, 2013, Line 3, INTERVENOR YAN (VICKY) XIONG's Motion To Quash Notice Of Levy And Or Recover. Denied. Xiong does not deny that the bank account was held jointly in her name and that of judgment debtor, and thus the levy was properly issued. Xiong failed to file a timely claim of exemption. Even if the Court were to treat this motion as a third-party claim of exemption, Xiong has failed to show that any of the funds were exempt. None of the authority cited by Xiong shows that she is entitled to the relief she is seeking. rulings.Case Title: IN RE: MARY JO MACK
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 28, 2013, Line 1, PETITIONER MARY MACK'S Motion To Compel And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Grant the motion. No sanctions or attorneys fees are awarded. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: TIM LARSEN VS. SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 28, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANT HENRY ALVAREZ' MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The motion to strike is denied in its entirety. Defendant failed to comply with CRC 3.1322(a); the notice of motion does not "quote in full the portion sought to be stricken except where the motion is to strike an entire paragraph. . . ." In any event, Defendant has not established, on the face of the Complaint or from any judicially noticed matter, that the allegations (referred to by paragraph, page and line number) are subject to the attorney-client privilege. Plaintiff's allegations support a claim for punitive damages. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. DELTA AIR LINES, INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 28, 2013, Line 18, DEFENDANT DELTA AIR LINES, INC MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE. Granted as to Jennifer Archie. No opposition filed. The Court will sign the proposed order submitted with the moving papers.Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

Welin (527286). rulings.Case Title: LAZAR SHAPIRO et al VS. ALEXANDRA FRIEDMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 28, 2013, Line 23, Order To Show Cause. The hearing on the order to show cause is continued to June 27, 2013. The TRO remains in effect. Defendant Friedman's opposition to the OSC is due June 17, 2013. Plaintiff's reply is due June 21, 2013. All filings are to be served by e-mail if agreed or overnight delivery. All parties are to provide courtesy copies of their filings directly with Department 302 per SF Local Rule 2.6B. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
